The latest quarter’s higher sales, earnings and a small dividend lift reinforce that story, but do not materially change the near term focus on converting backlog efficiently. The biggest risk remains that recent strength in margins and order activity could ease if large utility, data center or LNG projects slow.

data.title: ATI Aerospace And Defense Growth Tests High-Margin Earnings Story
data.description:
• ATI (NYSE:ATI) reported strong fourth quarter results, supported by demand for aerospace and defense products.
  • The company highlighted expanded long term agreements tied to next generation jet engines and missile programs.
  • Management pointed to proprietary alloys as a key factor in higher margins and a shift in product mix.

ATI enters this update with its shares at $143.93 and a very large 5 year return, reflecting substantial compounding over that period. Over the past year the stock is up 135.8%, including gains of 7.8% over the past week and 15.7% over the past month, while year to date performance sits at 20.7%. These moves frame the latest quarter as one more data point investors can weigh against an already strong share price run.

The company is putting clear emphasis on defense related growth, longer term customer commitments, and higher margin proprietary products. For you as an investor, the key questions from here are how durable that demand in aerospace and defense proves to be, and how much further ATI can shift its mix toward these higher margin offerings in coming quarters.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for ATI by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on ATI.

2 things going right for ATI that this headline doesn't cover.

For you as an investor, the key takeaway from this update is the tension between ATI’s product mix story and its near term profit trends. Fourth quarter sales were roughly flat year on year at US$1,177.1 million versus US$1,172.7 million, yet net income in that period moved from US$137.1 million to US$96.6 million, with diluted EPS from continuing operations at US$0.69 compared to US$0.94. Over the full year, however, ATI reported higher sales of US$4,587.4 million versus US$4,362.1 million and higher net income of US$404.3 million compared to US$367.8 million, which suggests the broader direction for the year was positive even though the final quarter was softer on earnings.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The ATI Narrative

  • The focus on proprietary alloys, next generation jet engines and long term aerospace and defense agreements ties directly to the narrative that higher margin, contracted volumes can support more stable earnings across cycles.
  • The fourth quarter profit step down, despite resilient sales, is a reminder that execution on efficiency and capital intensive projects is not guaranteed to be smooth, which tests the thesis that margin strength alone will carry results.
  • The latest buyback tranche completion, and the pause in repurchases in late 2025, touches on capital allocation and balance sheet flexibility, an angle that is not fully explored in the existing narrative around contracts and capacity investments.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for ATI to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Reported fourth quarter net income and EPS were lower than the prior year period, which shows that higher margin products do not always translate into quarter on quarter earnings strength.
  • ⚠️ ATI relies heavily on aerospace and defense demand, and on a relatively concentrated set of customers, so shifts in ordering patterns or program timing at major peers like Howmet Aerospace or Precision Castparts could affect volumes.
  • 🎁 Full year sales and net income were higher than the previous year, supported by demand in next generation jet engines and missile programs, which reinforces the role of these end markets as important earnings drivers.
  • 🎁 Expanded long term agreements tied to proprietary alloys may give ATI better volume visibility and pricing power versus peers such as Carpenter Technology and Allegheny based competitors that lack the same contract depth.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to track how quickly ATI converts its contract wins and proprietary alloy positioning into consistent quarterly earnings, not just full year gains. That includes watching future commentary on capacity expansion, cost efficiency and any updates on aerospace and defense program ramps. It is also worth keeping an eye on capital allocation, including whether share repurchases resume and how that interacts with debt and investment needs.

data.title: Is Ascendis Pharma (ASND) Still Attractive After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Ascendis Pharma's share price still reflects fair value after its recent run, this article will walk through what the numbers are saying right now.
  • The stock last closed at US$216.32, with returns of 5.1% over the past 30 days and 50.1% over the past year, while the 7-day return of a 2.6% decline shows the ride has not been perfectly smooth.
  • Recent moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing interest in Ascendis Pharma as a Nasdaq-listed pharmaceuticals and biotech company. Investors are watching how its product pipeline and clinical progress translate into long term potential. This broader context helps explain why the share price has seen both shorter term pullbacks and stronger multi year performance, including a 3-year return of 85.4% and a 5-year return of 36.4%.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation check framework, Ascendis Pharma scores 4 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued on four checks. This sets us up to compare different valuation methods next and then look at an even more complete way to think about what that score really means for you.

Ascendis Pharma delivered 50.1% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Biotechs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Ascendis Pharma Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes the cash Ascendis Pharma is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business could be worth right now.

Using Simply Wall St's 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, Ascendis Pharma's latest twelve month free cash flow is about €47.3m. Analysts contribute detailed projections out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year estimated at €1,355.0m, and further years extrapolated by Simply Wall St rather than by analysts directly.

When those projected cash flows, including the ten year path from 2026 to 2035, are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of €833.1 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$216.32, this implies the stock is assessed as 74.0% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Ascendis Pharma is undervalued by 74.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Ascendis Pharma.

Approach 2: Ascendis Pharma Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are not a steady guide, the P/S ratio is often more useful because it compares the share price with the revenue the business is already generating. It still reflects what the market is willing to pay for each unit of sales, and that tends to move with expectations for growth and perceived risk. Higher growth or lower risk can justify a higher “normal” P/S, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower one.

Ascendis Pharma currently trades on a P/S of 15.53x, compared with the Biotechs industry average of 11.28x and a peer group average of 24.95x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Ascendis is 13.36x. This is its proprietary estimate of what a balanced P/S might look like given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk factors. This Fair Ratio aims to give you a cleaner reference point than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company specific features.

Set against that Fair Ratio, Ascendis Pharma’s current 15.53x P/S is higher, which points to the stock being priced above this modelled fair level on a sales based view.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Assessing Erasca (ERAS) Valuation After Early Clinical Signals And Strong Share Price Momentum
data.description:
Advertisement

Conference spotlight and early clinical signals

Erasca (ERAS) is attracting attention after recent disclosures on early clinical signals for ERAS-0015 and ERAS-4001, alongside plans to present at Guggenheim’s Emerging Outlook Biotech Summit on February 12.

The company reported partial responses in several tumor types for ERAS-0015 during dose escalation without dose-limiting toxicities, and outlined Phase 1 data timelines that investors are watching ahead of further conference updates.

See our latest analysis for Erasca.

Erasca’s share price has had a volatile but strong run, with a 24.74% 1 month share price return, a 318.12% 3 month share price return, and a very large 1 year total shareholder return of 7x, as recent clinical updates and upcoming conferences keep growth potential and risk perceptions in sharp focus.

If this kind of biotech momentum has your attention, it could be a good time to see what else is moving in cutting edge medicine through our 25 healthcare AI stocks.

With Erasca now up more than 7x over the past year and trading close to its average analyst price target, the key question is whether recent enthusiasm has run ahead of itself or if the market is still underestimating its prospects.

Price to book of 10.7x, is it justified?

Erasca finished the last session at $12.00, and on a P/B of 10.7x it trades well above both its biotech peers and the wider US Biotechs industry.

P/B compares a company’s market value with its net assets. It is often used for early stage or unprofitable businesses where earnings-based ratios are less useful. For Erasca, the current 10.7x figure sits against a backdrop of no meaningful revenue, a net loss of $127.686m, and forecasts that still point to unprofitable operations over the next three years.

That combination suggests investors are placing a substantial premium on the company’s RAS/MAPK focused pipeline rather than its present financials. With earnings expected to decline by an average of 8.4% per year and no revenue forecast for next year, the market is effectively paying up for potential future outcomes that are not yet reflected in cash flows or profitability.

Compared to the US Biotechs industry average P/B of 2.6x, Erasca’s 10.7x multiple is more than four times higher, and it also stands above the 6.2x peer average. This kind of gap can persist if confidence in the pipeline stays strong, but it also leaves little room for disappointment if trial data or funding conditions turn out differently from current expectations.

data.title: Are Investors Undervaluing Ramelius Resources Limited (ASX:RMS) By 33%?
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Using the 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity, Ramelius Resources fair value estimate is AU$6.77
  • Ramelius Resources is estimated to be 33% undervalued based on current share price of AU$4.52
  • Our fair value estimate is 29% higher than Ramelius Resources' analyst price target of AU$5.26

Does the February share price for Ramelius Resources Limited (ASX:RMS) reflect what it's really worth? Today, we will estimate the stock's intrinsic value by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them to today's value. The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model is the tool we will apply to do this. Models like these may appear beyond the comprehension of a lay person, but they're fairly easy to follow.

We generally believe that a company's value is the present value of all of the cash it will generate in the future. However, a DCF is just one valuation metric among many, and it is not without flaws. If you still have some burning questions about this type of valuation, take a look at the Simply Wall St analysis model.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Step By Step Through The Calculation

We use what is known as a 2-stage model, which simply means we have two different periods of growth rates for the company's cash flows. Generally the first stage is higher growth, and the second stage is a lower growth phase. To start off with, we need to estimate the next ten years of cash flows. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

A DCF is all about the idea that a dollar in the future is less valuable than a dollar today, and so the sum of these future cash flows is then discounted to today's value:

10-year free cash flow (FCF) forecast

2026202720282029203020312032203320342035 Levered FCF (A$, Millions) AU$156.5mAU$399.2mAU$596.3mAU$636.4mAU$673.0mAU$707.1mAU$739.5mAU$771.0mAU$802.0mAU$832.9mGrowth Rate Estimate SourceAnalyst x3Analyst x4Analyst x4Est @ 6.73%Est @ 5.75%Est @ 5.07%Est @ 4.59%Est @ 4.25%Est @ 4.02%Est @ 3.85% Present Value (A$, Millions) Discounted @ 7.9% AU$145AU$343AU$474AU$469AU$460AU$448AU$434AU$419AU$404AU$389

("Est" = FCF growth rate estimated by Simply Wall St)
Present Value of 10-year Cash Flow (PVCF) = AU$4.0b

We now need to calculate the Terminal Value, which accounts for all the future cash flows after this ten year period. The Gordon Growth formula is used to calculate Terminal Value at a future annual growth rate equal to the 5-year average of the 10-year government bond yield of 3.5%. We discount the terminal cash flows to today's value at a cost of equity of 7.9%.

Terminal Value (TV)= FCF2035 × (1 + g) ÷ (r – g) = AU$833m× (1 + 3.5%) ÷ (7.9%– 3.5%) = AU$19b

Present Value of Terminal Value (PVTV)= TV / (1 + r)10= AU$19b÷ ( 1 + 7.9%)10= AU$9.0b

The total value, or equity value, is then the sum of the present value of the future cash flows, which in this case is AU$13b. In the final step we divide the equity value by the number of shares outstanding. Compared to the current share price of AU$4.5, the company appears quite good value at a 33% discount to where the stock price trades currently. The assumptions in any calculation have a big impact on the valuation, so it is better to view this as a rough estimate, not precise down to the last cent.

Important Assumptions

The calculation above is very dependent on two assumptions. The first is the discount rate and the other is the cash flows. You don't have to agree with these inputs, I recommend redoing the calculations yourself and playing with them. The DCF also does not consider the possible cyclicality of an industry, or a company's future capital requirements, so it does not give a full picture of a company's potential performance. Given that we are looking at Ramelius Resources as potential shareholders, the cost of equity is used as the discount rate, rather than the cost of capital (or weighted average cost of capital, WACC) which accounts for debt. In this calculation we've used 7.9%, which is based on a levered beta of 1.052. Beta is a measure of a stock's volatility, compared to the market as a whole. We get our beta from the industry average beta of globally comparable companies, with an imposed limit between 0.8 and 2.0, which is a reasonable range for a stable business.

    Dividend information for RMS.

  • Good value based on P/E ratio and estimated fair value.
Threat
  • Revenue is forecast to grow slower than 20% per year.
    What else are analysts forecasting for RMS?

Next Steps:

Although the valuation of a company is important, it is only one of many factors that you need to assess for a company. It's not possible to obtain a foolproof valuation with a DCF model. Instead the best use for a DCF model is to test certain assumptions and theories to see if they would lead to the company being undervalued or overvalued. If a company grows at a different rate, or if its cost of equity or risk free rate changes sharply, the output can look very different. data.title: FB Financial Chief Accounting Shift Puts Southern States Integration In Focus
data.description:
• FB Financial Corporation (NYSE:FBK) announced a Chief Accounting Officer transition.
  • Lynn Joyce has been appointed to succeed Jonathan Pennington as Chief Accounting Officer.
  • Joyce previously served as CFO of Southern States Bancshares, which FB Financial recently acquired.

For investors following regional banks, this kind of leadership change sits at the core of how a company manages its books and internal controls. FB Financial, a banking group focused on commercial and consumer clients, now has a senior finance leader who already knows the acquired Southern States Bancshares business from the inside. This background may be relevant to ongoing integration work.

Investors may monitor how Joyce influences accounting policies, financial reporting practices, and internal processes as the combined organization continues to operate after the acquisition. Any updates in future filings or earnings materials regarding integration progress, cost structures, or one-time items linked to the deal could provide additional insight into how this transition is proceeding.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for FB Financial by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on FB Financial.

Does the team leading FB Financial have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

This Chief Accounting Officer transition sits right in the middle of FB Financial’s control and reporting framework, which matters for how you interpret future results. Jonathan Pennington is leaving for another institution, so this is not being framed as a board driven shake up. The board has moved quickly to name Lynn Joyce, who has a long banking career and direct experience as CFO of Southern States Bancshares before its acquisition by FB Financial. That mix of technical accounting experience and first hand knowledge of the acquired franchise could help keep integration work aligned with the group’s reporting, especially around purchase accounting, fair value marks, and any merger related adjustments.

How This Fits Into The FB Financial Narrative

  • The existing narrative highlights integration of Southern States Bank as a key driver. Placing its former CFO in the Chief Accounting Officer role could support cleaner reporting on merger related items and future cost structure changes.
  • At the same time, any leadership turnover in a core finance role can challenge execution if there are differences in approach to provisioning, capital allocation reporting, or segment disclosures compared with prior management.
  • The narrative focuses on dividends, buybacks, and loan growth. This leadership change may not yet be reflected in expectations around integration timelines or one time expenses related to systems and process alignment.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Turnover in a senior accounting role can create short term execution risk if there are changes in how the bank approaches reserves, internal controls, or disclosure practices.
  • ⚠️ Integration of Southern States Bancshares remains a key task, and any misalignment between finance teams during this transition could affect how merger related costs or synergies are reflected in reported numbers.
  • 🎁 Joyce brings decades of CFO level experience at regional banks, which may support continuity in financial reporting and oversight of credit quality, similar to peers such as Pinnacle Financial Partners and First Horizon.
  • 🎁 Her familiarity with Southern States could help the combined bank identify efficiencies or balance sheet adjustments that might not be as visible to a newcomer.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch upcoming quarterly filings and commentary for any shifts in how FB Financial discusses credit costs, merger related items, and cost discipline as Joyce steps into the role. Pay particular attention to disclosures tied to the Southern States acquisition, including one time charges, integration milestones, and any refinements in segment reporting.
